_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: Exceptions
summary: Report failures across function calls without losing cleanup.
refresher: Throw an exception when a function cannot fulfill its contract and the caller may recover at a higher level. Catch exceptions by const reference, handle only cases you understand, and preserve useful error details.

blocks.body: An exception separates the normal return path from a failure path that may cross several function calls. It is useful when a function has no valid result and returning a special number would be unclear or easy to ignore.

Throwing creates an exception object and begins stack unwinding. Local objects in exited scopes are destroyed as control searches for a matching catch. This cleanup behavior works with RAII-managed resources and is a major reason to avoid raw ownership.

blocks.caption: The parser either returns a valid positive count or throws a descriptive exception that the caller handles.
A catch-all that does nothing hides the cause. Catch a meaningful type where a useful response is possible.

blocks.body: For the negative input, requirePositive checks its contract, constructs invalid_argument, and stops its normal path. Main catches that type by const reference, reads the message, and then continues after the catch block.

blocks.title: Exceptions are not ordinary branch logic
blocks.body: Use an if statement for expected choices such as an empty search result. Reserve exceptions for a function that cannot complete its promised operation, especially when several callers need the failure information.

blocks.prompt: How should standard exceptions usually be caught?
blocks.why: Catching by const reference avoids copying and preserves the dynamic exception information for the handler.

blocks.prompt: What happens to local RAII objects during stack unwinding?
blocks.why: As exception control exits each scope, destructors run for fully constructed local objects and release their resources.

blocks.title: Exceptions checklist
items: Catch only where recovery or context is possible.
Catch standard exceptions by const reference.
